description: Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. The command cannot be registered as a slash command in the Claude Code plugin index without a name. The plugin loader silently skips nameless commands on install.
suggested_fix: Add `name: audit-context` as the first field in the frontmatter block.

description: Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. Same effect as audit-context.md — this migration command cannot be registered or invoked. Both commands in the fabric-cli plugin are currently dead on install.
suggested_fix: Add `name: migrating-fabric-trial-capacities` as the first field in the frontmatter block.

evidence: Process steps describe output implicitly within numbered steps but no `## Output Format` heading exists
description: Agent has no explicit `## Output Format` section. The process steps describe what the agent produces implicitly, but consumers cannot rely on a stable output contract without a dedicated section.
suggested_fix: Add a `## Output Format` section that describes the written query file structure and any console output the agent emits.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples to illustrate distinct input contexts. The single example covers a basic R visual review but leaves more complex or edge-case scenarios undocumented.
suggested_fix: Add a second example that covers a different R visual scenario — for instance, a complex ggplot2 chart with multiple geom layers or a forecasting visual with statistical overlays.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. The deneb-reviewer agent in the same plugin has 2 examples; this agent is inconsistently documented within the custom-visuals/reports plugin.
suggested_fix: Add a second example covering a different SVG visual context — for instance, a multi-segment progress ring or a sparkline SVG measure with conditional coloring.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. Three of the four reviewer agents (r-reviewer, svg-reviewer, python-reviewer) share this gap; deneb-reviewer is the exception.
suggested_fix: Add a second example covering a more complex matplotlib/seaborn scenario — for instance, a multi-panel subplot or a statistical distribution plot with confidence bands.

description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Commands should declare which tools they may invoke so the runtime can enforce least-privilege constraints and users can audit the command's capabilities.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash]` to the frontmatter (or the precise set of tools the command actually uses).

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for rule description but provides no guard or prompt when invoked without arguments
description: No empty-input handling. If a user invokes `/suggest-rule` with no argument, the command will proceed without a rule description, producing a confusing or empty output. The command should detect empty `$ARGUMENTS` and ask for a description.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard at the start: check whether `$ARGUMENTS` is empty and, if so, prompt the user to describe the best practice they want to enforce before proceeding.

evidence: "appropriate Severity" appears twice in the rule generation workflow
description: Vague quantifier: "appropriate Severity" (×2). The command leaves severity assignment to judgment without specifying the mapping. This leads to inconsistent rule severity across teams.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete severity map: "Severity 3 (error) for violations that break the model or cause query failures; Severity 2 (warning) for quality or performance issues; Severity 1 (info) for documentation gaps."

description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. This issue compounds the missing `name` bug — the command is unregisterable AND underdeclared.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob]` to the frontmatter alongside the `name` fix.

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` but provides no guard when invoked without a workspace or scope argument
description: No empty-input handling. The audit-context command requires a workspace or item scope to be useful. Without an argument guard it will run a generic audit with no target.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard that prompts for a workspace name or Fabric item type when `$ARGUMENTS` is empty.

evidence: Frontmatter has `description` and `argument-hint` but no `model:` field
description: No `model:` frontmatter field. This multi-step migration command involves irreversible capacity operations; the runtime should route it to a capable model explicitly rather than inheriting a default.
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` (or the preferred tier) to the frontmatter alongside the `name` fix.

description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Compounds the missing `name` and missing `model` bugs — the command is triple-underdeclared.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read]` to the frontmatter alongside the other fixes.

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for capacity/workspace target but provides no guard for empty invocation
description: No empty-input handling. A capacity migration command that runs without a target argument could attempt to operate against an unintended scope. Empty-argument detection is especially important for irreversible operations.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard that requires the user to name the target capacity before executing any migration steps.
